title: Real Talk Ep. 8: What about aging?I’m not claiming to be old..
content:
Real Talk Ep. 8: What about aging?

I’m not claiming to be old, but at 36, I can scroll back through my camera roll from ten years ago and see the differences. We all age—whether it’s for the better or worse is really up to us.

Personally, I love where I’m at right now. I feel like I’m in my prime—youthful, strong, and a lot wiser than I was in my early twenties. Isn’t that the real peak? They say life begins at 40… so maybe the best is still ahead. We’ll see.

title: Real Talk Ep. 6: Weed. Every once in a while I get asked if ..
content: Real Talk Ep. 6: Weed. Every once in a while I get asked if I smoke weed. Even though I occasionally do, it is not something I necessarily recommend or, like for drinking alcohol, something I caution to do in moderation. And especially if you are under 25, I would not even try it. But as for most "vices", the key lies in moderation. I work out every day, eat freshly made food every single meal, never order takeout or eat processed food, I don't smoke cigarettes or consume significant amount of sugar. I can enjoy a few puffs of weed every once in a while, when I want to relax and calm my mind and body. It sometimes makes me creative. But it definitely slows you down if it becomes a habit, so be careful. Have a lovely Thursday everyone!

title: Real talk ep 5 - Skincare. When it comes to skin and hair ca..
content:
Real talk ep 5 - Skincare.

When it comes to skin and hair care, less is more. Stay fit and hydrated, eat clean and your body will take care of itself, including your skin and hair! I have been saying this for almost 15 years now, but I guess only now that I am a bit older I have some sort of proof that it works. In my family, for example, there is a lot of male hair loss, from a y0ung age. It could be just luck, but I believe the fact that I stopped putting chemicals on my hair 13 years ago has something to to with the fact that I still have a head full of hair... and as far as my skin goes, I guess I am still y0ung.. but I have also recently seen some my school mates and well.. let's just say that even if anecdotal I am happy with the way I look :) I do not edit any of my photos

title: Real talk Ep. 3 - Mezcal!   Does dr1nking alcohol have any h..
content:
Real talk Ep. 3 - Mezcal!

Does dr1nking alcohol have any health benefits? It seems like most studies agree that it doesn’t .. and yet if you enjoy it like I do, then surely that is benefit enough! As long as it doesn’t become a habit you can’t live without, and as long as you keep it moderate. For me also the type of alcohol you choose makes a difference.. the cleaner being Mezcal in my opinion. I love the taste and also the fact that it makes me feel good while dr1nking it with no repercussions the day after!

title: Real Talk - Ep.2 - Supplements. Let's settle this question. ..
content: Real Talk - Ep.2 - Supplements. Let's settle this question. You actually do not need supplements. Even the one supplement which I found to be the most effective - Creatine - Is not necessary. With the right food, sleep and exercise I have achieved and maintained the same results without. All the studies out there, the long podcasts, the articles.. who is paying for all of those? I suspect the supplement industry.. Happy Hump Day everyone!

title: Happy Monday! Let's start off with some motivation to go har..
content: Happy Monday! Let's start off with some motivation to go hard this week. Squatting 310 lbs in the 95 deg Miami heat. I find that while it is easier to warm up (obviously) overall I can perform better and get more done in a cooler environment (e.g. the gym). But it is good to give your body new challenges, plus it is so nice to be outside!!
